Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (361-365 Brannan Street, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington in Propria Persona.

Ms. Harrington explained the difficulty in getting the properties inventoried and in getting a realtor to sell them. There were three Petitions to Confirm Sale of Real Property on calendar for this matter. 1)The sale was confirmed at $57,140.00 for an undivided 1.4285% fractional interest in the 233-261 Ellis Street property. 2)The sale was confirmed at $94,229.00 for an undivided 2.5% fractional interest in the 361-365 Brannan Street property. 3)The sale was confirmed at $240,000.00 for an undivided 3.801% fractional interest in the 1670-1690 Folsom Street property. All sales will be subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the reports of sale. The Court granted the petitions (subject to receipt and review of the orders). Bond is waived on all three petitions.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293136 Estate of Elfriede Erika Preger.

Appearances: Ingrid Hsia for Gudrun Schappi.

Ms. Hsia reported that they reached a settlement, stated that the settlement agreement had been signed and that she would be filing a petition for approval of the settlement agreement. The Court advised Ms. Hsia that the two cases would be consolidated. Case number 293136 will be consolidated with case number 292711. All papers are to be filed in case number 292711 (lead case). Both matters were continued to May 18, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Continued To Apr-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition For Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator. Michelle McGill for and with Roseanna Hughes. Amy Harrington in Propria Persona. Ingrid Hsia for Gudrun Schappi.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to what authority the Court has to apoint a Special Administrator. The Court appointed Amy Harrington as the Special Administrator of the Estate of Elfriede Erika Preger to serve with general powers until further order of the Court based upon the agreement of Ms. McGill and Ms. Hsia. Ms. Harrington will prepare and submit the order. Bond is waived. The Court will consolidate Ms. Hsia's petition with this matter at the hearings on the general petition for both matters.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Ex Parte Petition For Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Continued To Jan-25-10 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington in Propria Persona. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court expressed concern with Ms. McGill and Ms. Hsia not being present, the conflict of Ms. Hughes, raised the issue of who had priority, who had authority to nominate, the IRA account, eligibility for appointment as well as many other conerns. The matter was continued to January 25, 2010 at 11:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Harrington will send notice to the Parties who reside out of the country.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-13-2010 Continued To Mar-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michell McGill for and with Roseanna Hughes (Petitioner). Ingrid Hsia for and with Gudrun Schappi (Respondent). Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Amy Harrington in Propria Persona. Kevin Hughes (Second Executor Nominee) in Propria Persona.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to which will the Court should be consider (2002 Will or 2003 Lost Will), who has priority to serve, who has authority to nominate, declinations being signed/not signed and conflict of interest. Mr. Ferdon explained that he declined to file a petition on behalf of the Public Administrator due to the back and forth going on between Orange County and San Francisco, and stated that the Court could appoint the SFPA on the existing petition if needed. The Parties were directed to step out of the courtroom and discuss the matter. Upon returning to the courtroom, the Parties stated that they had agreed on having Amy Harrington appointed with full authority including authority to deal with Creditors and to serve without bond. In addition, they agreed that Ms. Hsia would file the appropriate petition offering the 2003 will and stated that they would be amenable to mediation. The matter was continued to March 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel was directed to file an ex parte petition to have Ms. Harrington appointed as the Special Administrator. Ms. Hsia's petition is to be heard on March 24, 2010 as well. The Court referred to Probate Code 8440 and pointed out that Mr. Hughes and Ms. Hughes would need to decline and that the next person in line was the Public Administrator. Ms. Harrington argued that she believed that any person entitled to letters had authority to nominate. The Court will look into this matter.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Jan-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ingrid Hsia for Gudrun Schappi (Beneficiary). Edward Otonello specially appearing for Michelle McGill for Roseanna Hughes.

This was a pre-granted matter. Ms. Hsia requested time to respond stating that she was just hired Saturday. Mr. Otonello argued that the matter had been continued twice and that Gudrun Schappi had a previous Lawyer. The matter was continued to January 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give Counsel time to respond.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
